name: St James Loop
description: Backcountry loop with mixed roads, tracks, and hut access options.
difficultyExplanation: St James is physically solid but generally predictable for riders with some multi-day experience. It is less technical than hard alpine routes, yet still exposed enough to require planning.
beAwareOf: Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress.
tips: Start early, carry a weather margin, and keep backup navigation plus an extra food and water buffer.
trickiestSection: The Ada Pass section is exposed in bad weather and the gap from St James homestead to Hanmer Springs is the longest resupply stretch on the loop.
routeSummary: St James Loop is a intermediate bikepacking route covering about 93.7 km, commonly ridden over 3 days. Backcountry loop with mixed roads, tracks, and hut access options. Riders can expect 55% Gravel / 25% Road / 20% Track as the route unfolds.

Good planning makes this route far more enjoyable. The best riding window is generally Nov-Apr, with resupply described as Carry enough food and water for longer gaps between settlements; verify opening hours before committing to remote sections.. Overnight options are typically A mix of camping, huts, and local stays depending on your pace and daily target distance..

The key section to manage is: The Ada Pass section is exposed in bad weather and the gap from St James homestead to Hanmer Springs is the longest resupply stretch on the loop. Keep a close eye on Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress., especially if conditions change during the day.
recommendedSetupText: For this 3-day route, we recommend a Gravel Bike to balance comfort and control across mixed terrain. This setup keeps key gear stable and easy to access over long days.
